notes: Candidate diagnosed failure modes exist, but no single future objective is selected.

evidence_source: Phase 31 static-mask recovery and Phase 32 design
notes: Recovered masks exist, but the selected pilot target region is not fixed.
Phase 27 and Phase 29 masked baselines are available; full Phase 25/27/29 acceptance and rejection comparisons are not written.
Acceptance thresholds remain undeclared, so training is not authorized.
Rejection thresholds remain undeclared, including hard rejection for repeating the Phase 29 trade-off.
Standard guardrail metric group is defined; numeric thresholds are still separate readiness blockers.
Valid-domain masked guardrails are defined at Level 4+ proxy scope.
Boundary-ring guardrails are defined without claiming boundary flux closure.
High-impervious guardrails are defined as static-proxy diagnostics.
Manhole-region guardrails are defined as sparse-indicator proxy diagnostics.
Dry-threshold guardrails are defined, but exact threshold acceptance/rejection values remain unset.
The reviewed materials preserve the Level 4+ / Level 5 boundary.
The option set targets false-dry, false-wet, and dry-threshold failure modes rather than underresponse only.
The review rejects optimizing volume proxy alone because Phase 29 worsened RMSE, MAE, false-dry, false-wet, false-dry volume-loss, and false-wet volume-excess.
Conclusions are limited to Level 4+ masked physical proxy diagnostics.
